codeReview.summary: The feature wires a real MiniMax-backed processor into the worker and covers basic request, parsing, retry, and audit paths, but two milestone requirements are still not met: successful runs drop provider latency/token telemetry, and parser failures are surfaced as terminal run errors instead of producing the required parser-error result path.

issues.description: `ProcessRun` updates the run with `UpdateReviewRunStatus`, which only writes `status`, `error_code`, and `error_detail`. The scheduler later marks the run completed with `provider_latency_ms=0` and `provider_tokens_total=0`, so successful provider executions never persist the actual latency/token metrics captured in `response.Latency` and `response.Tokens`. This breaks the feature requirement to preserve provider execution details after the real review pipeline runs.

When provider JSON cannot be recovered, `MiniMaxProvider.Review` returns `scheduler.NewTerminalError(parser_error, parseErr)`. `ProcessRun` treats that as a generic provider failure and the scheduler marks the run failed, instead of returning a structured `ReviewResult` with `status=parser_error` / `summary_note` so downstream fallback handling can emit the parser-error note required by the milestone.

The parser fallback tests stop at asserting `ParseReviewResult` returns an error for unrecoverable input; there is no processor- or scheduler-level test proving parser failures become the expected parser-error review result rather than a failed run. This gap allowed the missing parser fallback behavior to ship.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker now hard-codes platform defaults (`IncludePaths: ["src/**"]`, `ExcludePaths: ["vendor/**"]`, confidence/severity thresholds, provider route) directly in `cmd/worker/main.go`, but the shared library/service docs only mention that platform defaults exist, not where runtime defaults are currently sourced from. Future workers could easily miss that the live worker is enforcing these hard-coded review-scope defaults.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: cmd/worker/main.go:47-55 constructs `rules.PlatformDefaults` inline; .factory/library/integrations.md only says MiniMax uses Anthropic-compatible API semantics and omits this runtime default-policy location.

summary: Reviewed commit `0cd5a87c2f30971bb212b506e33a7b8eac507ba4`, the handoff, transcript skeleton, worker skill, and the llm/worker diff. The implementation is close, but it does not persist actual provider latency/token metrics on success and it handles unrecoverable parser failures as failed runs instead of the milestone’s required parser-error result path, so the feature should not pass scrutiny yet.
